signals.text: CEO Aisha raised cost objections on Jan 26 call - FERMAT cost sometimes exceeds incremental revenue, net loss over 2.5 years
Email capture rate 20-30% lower on FERMAT vs Replo pages per Aisha's feedback
Contract expired Feb 20, 2026 - renewal discussion with new flat-fee pricing was proposed but outcome unclear
Key contact Emma has left Moment; new person took over but engagement has dropped
No upcoming calls scheduled with Moment on Pulkit's calendar
Granola notes flag signup/coupon code issues and need to move to $2,000/month pricing structure
Payment had cleared and a proactive follow-up email was planned per Granola standup notes

recent.title: Moment / FERMAT
recent.participants: Mia DyBuncio, Isabel O'Keefe, Aisha Chottani
recent.summary: Aisha Chottani (CEO) indicated that despite FERMAT pages converting slightly better than Replo, the cost structure (platform fee + 3% take rate) makes the partnership unprofitable. Isabel proposed a flat-fee model removing the take rate. Aisha is open to exploring if economics become favorable. Contract expires Feb 20, 2026.
action_items: Isabel to send new pricing proposal removing 3% take rate by EOD
Confirm correct billing email on file
Share copy of current contract expiring Feb 20, 2026

recent.title: Moment / FERMAT (No-show)
recent.summary: Scheduled meeting with Moment CEO Aisha did not take place as she failed to join. Internal discussion only. Decision made to reschedule.
action_items: Isabel to email to reschedule meeting

recent.title: Fermat x Moment
recent.summary: Emma consulted on Black Friday offers setup including 20% off first-purchase for Easy Energy and tiered BFCM discounts. Discussed FERMAT Forever links strategy. Hero 'Better than Booze' page showed 3%+ conversion rate. Identified longevity and anti-aging themed page opportunities.
action_items: Review BFCM sale pages and provide recommendations
Check with team on preventing double discount code use
Connect with Moment's new agency once selected

calls.analysis: Moment's most recent external call (Jan 26) was a critical retention discussion where CEO Aisha raised significant cost concerns. The brand has been comparing FERMAT to Replo and finding FERMAT's economics unfavorable. Key contact Emma has left, and there is a large gap in call activity. A Granola meeting on Mar 18 is the only recent touchpoint.

slack.analysis: No Slack MCP available for this refresh.

messages.subject: Everyone's fighting over BFCM, smart brands are winning on Earth Day
messages.snippet: Earth Day strategy playbook with funnel ideas, GWP strategies, and eco-brand collaboration concepts

messages.subject: Your ads were approved
messages.snippet: Your ads were approved and should begin delivering shortly or when scheduled

email.analysis: No direct email communication with Moment brand contacts was found in the past 30 days. All Gmail results were internal FERMAT newsletters (Earth Day playbook from Alka) and Meta ad approval notifications. This indicates zero active email engagement with the Moment team, which is concerning for account health.
